name: Conn of Hundred Battles
description: Conn Cétchathach, meaning Conn of the Hundred Battles, was a legendary High King of Ireland who reigned, according to traditional chronology, from 123 to 157 AD. He is a pivotal figure in Irish history and mythology, representing the archetype of a warrior-king and a symbol of Irish sovereignty. Conn's reign was marked by constant warfare, primarily against rebels and invaders, earning him his famous epithet. He is credited with uniting much of Ireland under his rule, establishing a relatively stable, if turbulent, kingdom. His most famous victory was the Battle of Maigh Lena against Éoghan Mór, king of Munster, a conflict that defined the political landscape of Ireland for centuries. Conn's lineage is traced back to the Milesians, the mythical ancestors of the Irish people, solidifying his claim to the throne. He is considered the ancestor of the Connachta dynasty, who held power in the west of Ireland for many generations. His legacy is one of military prowess, political ambition, and the consolidation of Irish identity in the face of external threats. Conn's stories are preserved in the Ulster Cycle and other early Irish texts, showcasing his bravery, strategic brilliance, and the challenges he faced in maintaining his kingdom. He is a central figure in the historical narratives of Ireland, embodying the spirit of resistance and the enduring struggle for Irish independence.
subtitle: High King, Warrior, Unifier of Ireland
achievements: Won the Battle of Maigh Lena
companions: None specifically named in most accounts, but loyal warbands

keyMyths.title: The Battle of Maigh Lena
keyMyths.description: Conn fought Éoghan Mór, king of Munster, in a decisive battle that shaped the political landscape of Ireland. Conn's victory established his dominance and defined the boundaries between the north and south. The battle is a key event in Irish history.

keyMyths.title: The Prophecy of Conn's Reign
keyMyths.description: Druids prophesied Conn's reign would be marked by constant battles and that he would be a great king. This prophecy underscored his destiny as a warrior-ruler and a defender of Ireland. It also speaks of his success in battle.

keyMyths.title: The Founding of the Connachta
keyMyths.description: Conn is considered the progenitor of the Connachta, a powerful dynasty that ruled the western province of Ireland for centuries. His descendants played a crucial role in Irish history, solidifying his lasting legacy. This lineage stems from his sons and their descendants.

sources.author: Geoffrey Keating
sources.work: Foras Feasa ar Éirinn (The History of Ireland)
sources.description: A comprehensive history of Ireland, including accounts of Conn's reign and battles.
sources.text: A comprehensive history of Ireland, including accounts of Conn's reign and battles.

sources.author: Annals of the Four Masters
sources.work: Annals of the Kingdom of Ireland
sources.description: A detailed chronological record of Irish history, mentioning Conn's key events.
sources.text: A detailed chronological record of Irish history, mentioning Conn's key events.
